name: Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)
shortDescription: An Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) (Field-Programmable Gate Array) is a reconfigurable chip of logic blocks and interconnects programmed by the user.
whatIsIt: An FPGA (Field-Programmable Gate Array) is a reconfigurable chip of logic blocks and interconnects programmed by the user.
whyExists: FPGAs give hardware-level parallelism and reconfigurability without fabricating an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC).
whereUsed: Used in signal processing, prototyping, and high-speed interfaces.
